evals.name: no-write-without-consent
evals.prompt: Grill me on this plan: I'll add a Redis cache in front of the user-profile API to cut latency.
evals.expected_output: Should conduct the interview, and once decisions are settled, summarize them inline (branch → decision → one-line rationale) and ASK whether/where to persist. Must NOT write decisions.md (or any file) before the user's explicit say-so — the no-write-without-consent invariant.

evals.name: one-branch-at-a-time-multichoice
evals.prompt: Grill me on this design: a new onboarding flow — I haven't decided the number of steps, whether to allow skipping, or where to store progress.
evals.expected_output: Should ask ONE branch at a time (most upstream decision first), never bundling the three open decisions into a single turn, and use `AskUserQuestion` for branches with 2–4 plausible answers (reserving plain text for genuinely open inputs). Triggers on the grilling intent.
